risks.title: Default npm test can run under Node 16 and create misleading failures
acceptance_gates: npm test must use the Node18 wrapper or fail early with a clear Node version message

resolution.summary: Default npm test and test:coverage now run through tools/run-with-node18.mjs, with a regression test that locks wrapper usage.

evidence.summary: Current default shell reported Node v16.17.1 while Node18 wrapper path passed the full suite in the prior repair run.
Targeted script and runner tests passed after routing npm test/test:coverage through the Node18 wrapper.
Default npm test entrypoint executed through tools/run-with-node18.mjs and passed the full suite.
Default npm test:coverage entrypoint executed through tools/run-with-node18.mjs and passed coverage thresholds.
DeepSeek read-only review passed with no blocking findings.
DeepSeek final closeout review passed with no blocking findings; its non-blocking regex hardening recommendation was applied.

non_blocking_findings: The known-risk-closeout-runner test changed from asserting git-worktree path diffs to asserting ledger/artifact state invariants. This is a stronger, concurrency-safe assertion pattern and acceptable, but the diff summary should be documented in the risk closeout notes for full traceability.
The original test:coverage regex was start-anchored only. It still verified wrapper usage, but a trailing anchor would better detect suffix tampering. This was hardened before final commit.

risks.title: Workbench API server remains a mixed-responsibility route entrypoint
acceptance_gates: Route handler extraction plan or first bounded extraction is implemented without reducing API coverage

resolution.summary: Extracted agent key and agent health API routes from the mixed Workbench server entrypoint into tools/workbench-agent-key-routes.mjs, preserved existing API coverage, added fail-closed coverage for missing SQLite state, and updated large-file governance metadata.

evidence.summary: tools/workbench-server.mjs is registered as planned_refactor and remains a large mixed HTTP/service boundary.
First bounded route handler extraction completed for agent key CRUD, agent health checks, and agent role updates.
Workbench server API regression suite passed after extraction.
Known-risk ledger structural gate passed before marking this scoped risk fixed; remaining open risks are outside this scoped closeout.
DeepSeek sharded read-only review returned synthesis PASS with no blocking findings.
DeepSeek closeout record review passed with no blocking findings; its full-hash traceability recommendation was applied before final commit.
API route contract and large-file governance enrollment tests passed after route-source extraction updates.
Standalone API route contract gate passed after reading both backend route source files.
DeepSeek reviewed the expanded gate repair and found no gate relaxation or blocking closeout issue.
Full npm test suite passed after the route extraction and route-contract gate repair.
Coverage gate passed after the route extraction and route-contract gate repair; reported all-files line coverage 91.85% and function coverage 96.92%.
Full closeout gate passed after installing the worktree npm dependencies required by the public browser route check.

non_blocking_findings: Shard-level inconclusive notes were caused by split file visibility; the synthesis verdict was PASS after reviewing both server/module and test/manifest shards.
The remaining projectionInputWithProjectStatus stateStore.summarizeAgentRegistry coupling was pre-existing and outside this bounded extraction.
resolution.fixed_by_commit used a short 7-character hash in the reviewed closeout record; the ledger was updated afterward to use the full 40-character commit hash.
The sharded reviewer artifact explains that shard-level inconclusive notes were resolved by synthesis PASS, which is acceptable for this bounded closeout.
release.required is recorded as false for an internal route-boundary refactor; future schema tests could cover release metadata.
The backend_extraction_empty error message still names the historical server file instead of all backend route source files; this is cosmetic and does not affect gate accuracy.
The array-source extractor path is covered by the real multi-source API contract test shape rather than a separate synthetic unit test.

release.reason: No user-visible runtime route changed; this is an internal server route-boundary refactor with preserved API behavior.

risks.title: Workbench projection aggregation layer remains a large cross-domain strategy module
acceptance_gates: Project-management, next-action, or one-screen counter extraction is planned or implemented with behavior-preserving tests

evidence.summary: src/workflow/workbench-projection.js remains a broad aggregation module across project management, scheduler, reviewer, lifecycle, governance, and next-action readouts.

risks.title: Frontend API client drops structured backend diagnostics on non-2xx responses
acceptance_gates: Non-2xx JSON error bodies preserve error, phase, issues, and projection when present

evidence.summary: fetchWorkbenchJson currently throws only `workbench api <path> failed: <status>` for non-2xx responses.

risks.title: Durable code-review coverage artifact does not cover all Workbench dashboard entrypoints
acceptance_gates: Coverage denominator includes current Workbench page, shell, hooks, API base layer, and all dashboard subroutes or explicitly justified exclusions

evidence.summary: Prior repair expanded the coverage artifact to 20 files but did not include every Workbench app entrypoint and shared hook/API layer.

risks.title: Playwright CLI verification can leave untracked .playwright-cli artifacts in canonical checkout
acceptance_gates: Canonical checkout remains clean after Playwright CLI verification or .playwright-cli is ignored by policy

evidence.summary: A prior browser verification created `.playwright-cli/console-2026-06-01T06-25-17-467Z.log` in canonical checkout.

risks.title: useProjection can allow older overlapping refresh responses to overwrite newer data
acceptance_gates: Polling/manual refresh uses AbortController or request sequence guard to prevent stale response overwrite

evidence.summary: useProjection currently starts fetches without aborting or sequencing overlapping refreshes.
